briefing:
## 🌍 Top 5 Global Fintech News

- **[Fintech founder charged with fraud; AI app found to be humans in the Philippines](https://techcrunch.com/2025/04/10/fintech-founder-charged-with-fraud-after-ai-shopping-app-found-to-be-powered-by-humans-in-the-philippines/)** — *Hacker News* — A fintech founder faces fraud charges after their supposedly AI-powered shopping app was revealed to be operated by human workers in the Philippines.

- **[Americans see their savings vanish in Synapse fintech crisis](https://www.cnbc.com/2024/11/22/synapse-bankruptcy-thousands-of-americans-see-their-savings-vanish.html)** — *Hacker News* — Thousands of Americans lost access to their savings following the collapse of fintech middleware firm Synapse, raising urgent questions about consumer protections in embedded finance.

- **[Fintech is making credit cards weirder](https://workweek.com/2022/08/05/fintech-is-making-credit-cards-weirder/)** — *Hacker News* — Fintech innovation is transforming the traditional credit card into a more complex, niche financial product with novel reward structures and underwriting models.

- **[Apple acquires UK open banking startup Credit Kudos](https://ffnews.com/newsarticle/apple-acquires-uk-open-banking-startup-credit-kudos/)** — *Hacker News* — Apple has acquired UK-based open banking startup Credit Kudos, signalling the tech giant's growing ambitions in financial data and credit decisioning.

- **[Finance ministers and top bankers raise serious concerns about Mythos AI model](https://www.bbc.com/news/articles/c2ev24yx4rmo?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=rss)** — *BBC Business* — Global finance leaders are sounding alarms over the Mythos AI model's unprecedented ability to identify and exploit cybersecurity vulnerabilities in financial systems.

---

## 🍁 Top 5 Canadian Fintech News

- **[Inside Wealthsimple's "simple" effort to bring Canadian talent home](https://betakit.com/inside-wealthsimples-simple-effort-to-bring-canadian-talent-home/)** — *BetaKit* — Canadian fintech leader Wealthsimple is actively recruiting diaspora talent back to Canada as it looks to deepen its domestic competitive advantage.

- **[Why the future of cross-border payments will be built through partnership](https://betakit.com/why-the-future-of-cross-border-payments-will-be-built-through-partnership/)** — *BetaKit* — A new analysis argues that collaboration between fintechs, banks, and infrastructure providers — not competition — will define the next era of cross-border payments innovation in Canada and beyond.

- **[Canada calls screen scraping 'unsecure,' sets Open Banking target for 2023](https://finledger.com/2021/08/09/canada-sets-open-banking-target-for-2023/)** — *Hacker News* — Canada officially labeled screen scraping as insecure and committed to an open banking framework, a foundational moment for the country's ongoing financial data reform journey.

- **[Carney's pitch to unlock trillions in global investment](https://www.cbc.ca/news/business/armstrong-invest-in-canada-summit-carney-9.7166810?cmp=rss)** — *CBC Business* — Prime Minister Carney is making a bold case to global investors to direct trillions in capital toward Canada, with implications for fintech and tech ecosystem funding.

- **[Canada opens applications to build a public AI supercomputer](https://betakit.com/canada-opens-applications-to-build-a-public-ai-supercomputer/)** — *BetaKit* — The federal government has opened applications for organizations to build a publicly accessible AI supercomputer, a move that could accelerate AI-driven fintech development across the country.

---

## 🌊 Bonus: Relevant to Atlantic Fintech's Focus (1–3 articles)

- **[Open Banking and Payments Competition](https://www.bitsaboutmoney.com/archive/open-banking-and-payments-competition/)** — *Hacker News* — A deep-dive into how open banking frameworks reshape payments competition, directly relevant to Atlantic Canada credit unions and fintechs preparing for Canada's open banking rollout.

- **[Fintech dystopia](https://fintechdystopia.com/)** — *Hacker News* — A critical look at the darker consequences of fintech's rapid growth, offering a timely counterpoint for ecosystem builders in Atlantic Canada focused on responsible financial inclusion.

- **[Wall Street Rushes to Price Out War Damage as Stocks Hit Records](https://financialpost.com/pmn/business-pmn/wall-street-rushes-to-price-out-war-damage-as-stocks-hit-records)** — *Financial Post* — As markets rebound on Iran ceasefire hopes, Atlantic Canadian fintechs and investors should note the shifting macroeconomic environment and its potential impact on venture capital appetite and consumer financial health.
